summary: First-run comprehensive testing of Vendor Tracker (JWT auth + MongoDB + Emergent object storage). 37/37 backend pytest cases pass. All frontend flows (login, dashboard KPIs, theme switch + persistence, vendor CRUD via UI, document upload, admin users CRUD, audit log, directory, settings 5-theme grid, queue) work end-to-end.

issues: Recharts logs benign width(-1)/height(-1) warnings when charts render before layout; not a real bug
CRA dev-only overlay may briefly surface 'ResizeObserver loop completed with undelivered notifications' — harmless, only in dev build

critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI on_event('startup'/'shutdown') hooks — consider migrating to lifespan context (non-blocking, but will warn on newer FastAPI).

CORS_ORIGINS='*' combined with allow_credentials=True — Starlette silently ignores credentials when origins is wildcard; if cookie-based auth is later added, this will break. For JWT Bearer it is fine today.

PUT /api/vendors/{id} accepts raw dict payload with no Pydantic validation, so any client-side bug could silently store unknown top-level keys. Consider a stricter model or explicit whitelist for fields.
Reset-password endpoint has no invalidation of previously issued JWTs (7-day TTL). Acceptable for MVP but worth a follow-up (token version / jti blacklist).
Storage init failure only logs a warning at startup; document upload will 500 later. Consider surfacing a health flag in GET /api/ so ops can detect degraded state.
server.py is ~640 lines — still manageable but starting to justify a split into routers/{auth,users,vendors,documents,analytics}.py.
test_credentials: admin@vendortracker.io / Admin@12345 (seeded). Tests create TEST_reviewer_* / TEST_approver_* users on the fly.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created several TEST_-prefixed users and a few 'TEST Vendor <uuid>' vendors + one 'Playwright Vendor <ts>' vendor via UI. No cleanup was done; safe to leave since they are prefixed and harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoints are stable and fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (run with `pytest -n 2 backend/tests`). Frontend Radix Select needs role='option' clicks (see Playwright script above). Document upload uses input[type=file] hidden inside labels with data-testid='upload-<catKey>'. Emergent object storage is functional (upload+download+delete verified). PWA manifest and sw.js are served. Theme persistence stores 'theme' field on user document and is read by frontend on mount.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional issues found in this first-run test.
